title: Jake Sullivan on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Jake Sullivan, former Director of the Policy Planning Office at the U.S. State Department and former National Security Adviser to Vice President Joe Biden, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about the secret negotiations behind the Iran nuclear deal, long-term policy planning at the State Department, and how to stay sharp on a demanding travel schedule.
body: Jake Sullivan is the former Director of the Policy Planning Office at the U.S. State Department and the former National Security Adviser to Vice President Joe Biden. Along with Bill Burns, Sullivan conducted secret negotiations with Iran that led to the P5+1 agreement. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article discusses Jake Sullivan's background as former Director of the Policy Planning Office at the U.S. State Department and former National Security Adviser to Vice President Joe Biden. It highlights his role in conducting secret negotiations with Iran alongside Bill Burns, which ultimately led to the P5+1 agreement. The content references his appearance on the Office Hours podcast and connects to broader discussions about diplomacy, Iran's nuclear program, and U.S. foreign policy. Additional context includes related analyses of regional dynamics, such as Hamas' strategic calculations and international reactions to strikes on Iran, suggesting ongoing debates about diplomatic approaches versus military actions in Middle Eastern conflicts.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了杰克·沙利文作为美国国务院政策规划办公室前主任及副总统乔·拜登前国家安全顾问的背景。文章重点强调了他与比尔·伯恩斯共同主导与伊朗的秘密谈判，这些谈判最终促成了P5+1协议。内容提及他在“办公时间”播客中的露面，并关联到有关外交、伊朗核计划及美国外交政策的更广泛讨论。文章还包含对地区动态的相关分析，例如哈马斯的战略考量以及国际社会对打击伊朗行动的反应，暗示了关于中东冲突中外交途径与军事行动之间的持续辩论。
